title: Assessment of Human Islet Composition and Acinar Cell Component by Immunofluorescence Staining
author: IIDP-HIPP
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2nfqdbn

steps.title: Preparation of Reagents for Immunofluorescence Staining of Islet Cryosections
steps.instruction: Preparation of Reagents for Immunofluorescence Staining of Islet Cryosections

steps.title: 10% Triton X-100 stock ( 30...
steps.instruction: 10% Triton X-100 stock ( 30 mL ) – combine 3 mL Triton-X-100 and 27 mL 1X PBS. Mix on shaker for 30 min or until Triton X-100 is completely dissolved and store at 4 °C for up to 1 month.

steps.title: Permeabilization Solution (0
steps.instruction: Permeabilization Solution (0.2% Triton, 50 mL ) – combine 1 mL of 10% Triton stock and 49 mL 1X PBS.

steps.title: Blocking Buffer (5% NDS, 4 mL ) – combine 0
steps.instruction: Blocking Buffer (5% NDS, 4 mL ) – combine 0.2 mL NDS and 3.8 mL 1X PBS.

steps.title: Antibody Buffer ( 10 mL ) – combine 0
steps.instruction: Antibody Buffer ( 10 mL ) – combine 0.1 g BSA, 0.1 mL 10% Triton stock, and 9.8 mL 1X PBS.

steps.title: DAPI staining solution (1:25,000, 50 mL...
steps.instruction: DAPI staining solution (1:25,000, 50 mL ) – combine 2 µL DAPI stock (5mg/mL) and 50 mL 1X PBS.

steps.title: Immunofluorescence Staining Procedure on Islet Cryosections
steps.instruction: Immunofluorescence Staining Procedure on Islet Cryosections

steps.title: Use freshly-made antibody incubation buffers, and wash buffers
steps.instruction: Use freshly-made antibody incubation buffers, and wash buffers. Steps 2.3, 2.4, 2.8, 2.10, 2.11 can be done in Kartell Staining Chambers.

steps.title: Let the frozen sections thaw at...
steps.instruction: Let the frozen sections thaw at room temperature and air-dry for about 30 minutes.

steps.title: Wash the sections with 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash the sections with 50 mL 1X PBS 3 times for 5 minutes to remove the OCT.

steps.title: Permeablize the tissue section with 0
steps.instruction: Permeablize the tissue section with 0.2 % Triton for 15 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Wash the tissue in 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash the tissue in 50 mL 1X PBS 3 times for 3-5 minutes.

steps.title: Draw circles or rectangles around the...
steps.instruction: Draw circles or rectangles around the sections with PAP marker and let them dry for about 5 minutes.

steps.title: Block the sections with 5% normal...
steps.instruction: Block the sections with 5% normal donkey serum (made from 100% stock)/1X PBS at room temperature for 90 minutes in a humidified chamber.

steps.title: Aspirate the blocking solution, add primary...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the blocking solution, add primary antibodies ( Table 1 ) diluted in 0.1% Triton-X-100 (made from 10% Triton stock)/1% BSA/1X PBS and incubate in a humidified chamber overnight at 4 °C . Table 1. List of primary and secondary antibodies for assessment of islet cell composition and endocrine/acinar cell composition Antibody Information Primary Antibodies: C-peptide (rat): GN-ID4 RRID:AB_2631151 Glucagon (mouse): ab10988 RRID:AB_297642 Glucagon (rabbit): 2760S RRID:AB_659831 Somatostatin (goat): sc-7819 RRID:AB_2302603 HPX1 (mouse): NBP1-18951, RRID:AB_1625456 Secondary Antibodies: Rat IgG-Cy2 (donkey): 712-225-150 RRID:AB_2340673 Rat IgG-Cy5 (donkey): 712-175-150 RRID:AB_2340671 Mouse IgG-Cy3 (donkey): 715-165-150 RRID:AB_2340813 Rabbit IgG-Cy5 (donkey): 711-175-152 RRID:AB_2340607 Goat IgG-Cy5 (donkey): 705-175-147 RRID:AB_2340415 Mouse IgG-Cy3 (donkey): 715-165-150 RRID:AB_2340813

steps.title: Aspirate the primary antibodies and wash...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the primary antibodies and wash the sections with 1X PBS three times for 10 minutes each.

steps.title: Add secondary antibodies ( Table 1 ) diluted in 0
steps.instruction: Add secondary antibodies ( Table 1 ) diluted in 0.1% Triton/1% BSA/1X PBS and incubate for 1.5 hours at room temperature in a humidified chamber.

steps.title: Aspirate the secondary antibody and counterstain...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the secondary antibody and counterstain slides with 1:25,000 DAPI/PBS for 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Remove from DAPI and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove from DAPI and wash the sections with 1X PBS three times for 15 minutes each.

steps.title: Mount the sections with SlowFade Gold mounting medium
steps.instruction: Mount the sections with SlowFade Gold mounting medium.

steps.title: Imaging and Analysis of Fluorescently Labeled Islet Cryosections
steps.instruction: Imaging and Analysis of Fluorescently Labeled Islet Cryosections

steps.title: Capture images of islet sections using...
steps.instruction: Capture images of islet sections using a high-resolution whole slide scanning system (ScanScope FL, Aperio/Leica) connected to a web-based digital slide repository powered by eSlide Manager and housed in the Vanderbilt University Medical Center data center ( examples of islet images are shown in Figures 1 and 2 ).

steps.title: Using a tissue classifier algorithm (Halo™,...
steps.instruction: Using a tissue classifier algorithm (Halo™, Indica Labs) analyze islet images (50 -100 islets/labeling experiment) to provide a quantitative assessment of the islet cell composition and endocrine/acinar cell compartments for a given human islet preparation ( examples of quantitative islet assessment are shown Figures 3 and 4 ).

steps.title: Data Storage and Reporting
steps.instruction: Data Storage and Reporting

steps.title: To facilitate data management and ensure...
steps.instruction: To facilitate data management and ensure data security, the Vanderbilt HIPP uses an institutional server-based platform for data storage and analysis.

steps.title: Upon analysis completion (within 14 business...
steps.instruction: Upon analysis completion (within 14 business days) annotated images containing metadata and image analysis outputs will be uploaded to the IIDP-HIPP database and immediately disseminated to IIDP-affiliated investigators and islet isolation centers.
